description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, consisting of a rectangular grid of photovoltaic panels supported by a metallic frame. The panels are dark, likely made of tempered glass with visible grid lines indicating individual solar cells. The supporting structure is composed of angled metal legs and crossbars, providing stability and elevating the panels above the ground. The base area beneath the structure is covered with a patterned surface, possibly for visual demarcation or to prevent vegetation growth. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for maximum sunlight exposure and durability in outdoor urban environments. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the views provided.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a metallic support frame and a grid of dark photovoltaic panels, elevated above a patterned base surface.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the solar panel array from a front-left angle, highlighting the angled metal supports and the patterned base beneath the structure.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the array from a front-right perspective, revealing the symmetry of the support legs and the full extent of the patterned ground area.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the array from a rear-left angle, emphasizing the back supports and the alignment of the solar panels.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a rear-right view, showing the grid arrangement of the panels and the supporting frame's structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled aluminum or steel frame
attribute_array_configuration: single rectangular grid of approximately 3x4 panels
